mnemonics: ACID Rain: Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, Durability - the essential properties that protect database integrity.
ACTIVE Transactions: Always Commit To Isolate, Validate, Execute - the lifecycle of a transaction.
keyPoints: A transaction is a single logical unit of work that accesses and possibly modifies the contents of a database.
The ACID properties (Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, Durability) ensure transaction reliability and data integrity.
System concepts like schedules, serializability, and concurrency control are used to manage the execution of concurrent transactions.
commonMistakes: Assuming a committed transaction's changes are immediately written to disk; they may exist only in memory buffers initially.
Confusing a consistent state (which a transaction must leave the DB in) with a correct state (which depends on the real-world semantics).
Believing that higher isolation levels always lead to better performance; they often increase locking and reduce concurrency.
